business_definition: A standardized insurance protection that compensates for defined insured risks.
A unique business identifier assigned to a coverage.
Official business name of the coverage.
Classification of the coverage according to its protection objective.
Business classification of the coverage.
Current operational status of the coverage.
Current lifecycle stage of the coverage.
Current version of the canonical coverage definition.
Business description explaining the protection provided.
Short business summary of the coverage.
Represents a benefit delivered by the coverage.
Official name of the benefit.
Detailed explanation of the benefit.
Monetary compensation offered under the benefit.
Non-financial assistance offered by the benefit.
Business event activating the benefit.
Party receiving the benefit.
Business priority of the benefit.
Availability status of the benefit.
Short explanation of the benefit.
Maximum protection available under a coverage.
Classification of the coverage limit.
Highest payable amount under the coverage.
Maximum cumulative payout during the policy period.
Maximum payout per insured event.
Specific lower limit applicable to selected benefits.
Indicates whether financial protection has no upper limit.
Business condition defining when limits apply.
Remaining unused protection available.
Simplified explanation of coverage limits.
Represents business conditions required for coverage applicability.
Classification of the condition.
Business description of the condition.
Condition that must always be satisfied.
Condition applicable only under selected circumstances.
Verification status of the condition.
Represents violation of a required condition.
Business importance of the condition.
Indicates whether the condition is actively enforced.
Simplified explanation of applicable conditions.
Defines where and when a coverage may be applied.
Vehicle categories eligible for the coverage.
Driver categories eligible for the coverage.
Customer segments for which the coverage is intended.
Geographic regions where the coverage is valid.
Vehicle usage types supported by the coverage.
Policy types that may include this coverage.
Indicates whether the coverage is currently available for sale.
Business rule governing activation of the coverage.
Business rule governing expiration of the coverage.
Represents the business rules determining whether a coverage may be applied.
Business rule defining eligibility for the coverage.
Minimum vehicle age required for the coverage.
Maximum vehicle age permitted for the coverage.
Minimum driver age required for the coverage.
Maximum driver age permitted for the coverage.
Represents whether eligibility has been verified.
Final decision regarding coverage eligibility.
Business explanation supporting the eligibility decision.
Simplified explanation of the eligibility requirements.
example: Applicable for vehicles up to 10 years old.

business_definition: Represents situations where a coverage does not provide protection.
Official name of the exclusion.
Classification of the exclusion.
Business description explaining the exclusion.
example: Claims while intoxicated are not covered.

business_definition: Exclusion required by regulation.
Insurer-specific exclusion that extends beyond regulatory requirements.
Business event activating the exclusion.
Business importance assigned to the exclusion.
Represents verification of exclusion applicability.
Simplified explanation of the exclusion.
Represents the financial responsibility retained by the insured before coverage applies.
Classification of deductible applied to the coverage.
Mandatory deductible applied to covered claims.
Optional deductible selected to reduce premium.
Combined deductible applicable to the coverage.
Defines when the deductible is applied.
Represents whether deductible waiver benefits are supported.
Simplified explanation of deductible rules.
Represents the operational lifecycle of a coverage definition.
Date on which the coverage became available for use.
Indicates whether the coverage is currently available.
Revision identifier of the canonical coverage definition.
Date on which the coverage is withdrawn from future products.
Current lifecycle classification of the coverage.
Coverage intended to replace the current coverage after retirement.
Indicates whether older policy versions may continue using this coverage.
Business reason for retiring or replacing the coverage.
Represents a business relationship between two canonical coverages.
Coverage that can coexist with another coverage.
Coverage requiring another coverage before activation.
Coverage that cannot coexist with another coverage.
Coverage that increases the value of another coverage.
Reusable collection of related canonical coverages.
AI-generated recommendation for combining multiple coverages.
Customer segment most suited for the coverage.
Vehicle segment for which the coverage is optimized.
Driver segment best matched to the coverage.
Canonical coverage recommended if the current coverage is unsuitable.
Natural-language explanation describing why the coverage is recommended.
example: Recommended due to high value and strong suitability.

business_definition: Date on which the latest coverage assessment was generated.
Version of the assessment methodology used.
Additional remarks associated with the latest assessment.
example: Highly recommended for urban private vehicles.

business_definition: Structured explanation of the factors influencing the recommendation.
example: High benefit-to-cost ratio with broad applicability.

business_definition: Timestamp representing the latest successful assessment execution.
Primary knowledge source used to define the canonical coverage.
